The match, set for October 2023, could mark a turning point in the league's history.

articles.title: Registrar’s dossier on fraud in Sh1bn Nyeri land adds twist
articles.summary: The dispute arose from the sale of a former colonial farm to hundreds of ex-workers.
articles.context: The land in question was part of the former White Highlands, historically owned by colonial settlers and later returned to the Kenyan government. In 2003, the government initiated the resettlement of landless Kenyans, including former farm workers, but disputes over ownership and compensation have persisted. The recent revelations from the registrar’s dossier add urgency to the ongoing conflict, highlighting the complexities of land reform in Kenya, particularly in light of the 2010 Constitution's provisions on land rights.

articles.title: Productivity is Kenya’s real edge, we must build on it
articles.summary: Kenya needs a national productivity board that treats human capital as infrastructure.
articles.context: Kenya's economy has faced significant challenges, including the impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic and ongoing political tensions following the contentious 2022 elections. The government's push for economic recovery emphasizes the need for enhanced productivity, particularly in light of the Vision 2030 development blueprint. As regional competition intensifies, particularly with Ethiopia's growing manufacturing sector, establishing a national productivity board could position Kenya to leverage its human capital effectively and sustain economic growth.

articles.title: Curse of want - Poverty undoes man
articles.summary: Poverty is not witchcraft or a misfortune. It is a managed outcome of political choices.
articles.context: In Kenya, the legacy of colonial land policies and the subsequent failure to implement equitable land reforms have entrenched poverty among marginalized communities. The 2010 Constitution aimed to address these disparities, yet ongoing political corruption and the recent fallout from the 2022 general elections have exacerbated economic inequalities. As the government grapples with rising inflation and unemployment, the plight of the impoverished is increasingly viewed as a direct consequence of these political failures.

articles.title: Betting industry investors face tougher permit requirements

The government's move to tighten licensing requirements follows a 2019 directive aimed at regulating the sector more stringently, which resulted in the closure of several betting firms. This shift reflects ongoing tensions between the government and betting companies, as well as broader societal debates about gambling's role in Kenyan culture and economy.

The event also aligns with Kenya's broader strategy to enhance its global sporting profile.

articles.title: Court orders disclosure of KPC privatisation records
articles.summary: Justice Patricia Nyaundi orders govt to produce valuation reports and other transaction papers...
articles.context: The Kenya Pipeline Company (KPC) has been at the center of a contentious privatization debate since the government announced plans to sell a stake in the state-owned entity in 2021. This move has faced opposition from various stakeholders, including labor unions and civil society, who argue it could undermine national interests. The court's order for disclosure of valuation reports comes amid heightened scrutiny over transparency and accountability in public asset management, particularly in light of ongoing corruption allegations.

articles.title: Former Trump adviser suggests recruiting volunteers from South Sudan, Uganda and other regions to defend Israel
articles.summary: Former U.S. President Donald Trump’s foreign policy adviser, Dr. Walid Phares, has suggested recruiting volunteers from South Sudan, Uganda and several other African countries and regions to help defend Israel amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East. In a message posted on the social media platform X on Tuesday, Phares stated that individuals from South […] The post Former Trump adviser suggests recruiting volunteers from South Sudan, Uganda and other regions to defend Israel appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The suggestion by Dr. Walid Phares comes amid heightened tensions in the Middle East, particularly following the October 2023 Hamas attack on Israel, which has escalated military responses and international discourse on security. South Sudan and Uganda, both grappling with their own internal conflicts and regional instability, have historically been involved in broader geopolitical dynamics, including alliances with Western nations and responses to Middle Eastern conflicts, making this recruitment proposal particularly controversial and significant.

articles.title: Gachagua urges Ol Kalou residents to return to polling stations, protect their votes
articles.context: The call by Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua for Ol Kalou residents to return to polling stations comes amid heightened tensions following the controversial August 2022 general elections in Kenya, where allegations of electoral fraud led to protests and unrest. The political landscape remains polarized, particularly between President William Ruto's Kenya Kwanza Alliance and the opposition led by Raila Odinga, complicating the electoral process and raising concerns about voter safety and integrity.

articles.title: Religious leader appeals for aid after UPDF displaces thousands in Kajo-Keji
articles.summary: The Episcopal Diocese of Liwolo in Kajo-Keji County, Central Equatoria State has appealed for urgent humanitarian assistance following recent armed hostilities that it says have displaced an estimated 10,000 people. In a public appeal, Bishop Joseph Abba of the Episcopal Church of South Sudan said thousands of civilians had fled their homes after attacks in […] The post Religious leader appeals for aid after UPDF displaces thousands in Kajo-Keji appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The recent displacement in Kajo-Keji follows escalating tensions between the South Sudanese government and armed opposition groups, particularly the Sudan People's Liberation Army-In Opposition (SPLA-IO). The area has been a flashpoint since the 2013 civil war, with ongoing clashes exacerbated by the presence of the Uganda People's Defence Force (UPDF) supporting the South Sudanese government. This situation is further complicated by the fragile peace agreement signed in 2018, which has struggled to fully stabilize the region.

articles.title: US Ambassador Touches Down in Gondar to Champion Trade, Regional Stability
articles.summary: Just a day after executing a high-stakes diplomatic mission to Mekelle in the Tigray region, US Ambassador Ervin Massinga arrived in Gondar today to advance commercial ties, prioritize regional peace, and review critical healthcare initiatives. Massinga transitioned from his discussions over Tigray’s fragile peace process to engagements with local and regional officials in the Amhara […]
articles.context: The visit comes amid ongoing tensions in Ethiopia, particularly following the November 2020 conflict in Tigray, which has left deep scars on regional stability. The U.S. has been actively involved in peace negotiations, notably the Pretoria Agreement signed in November 2022, aimed at ending hostilities. Ambassador Massinga's focus on trade and healthcare in Gondar is crucial as the Amhara region grapples with the aftermath of conflict and seeks to rebuild its economy and social services.

articles.title: Yakani urges South Sudanese to reject hate speech ahead of 2026 elections
articles.summary: Civil society leader Edmund Yakani has called on South Sudanese to refrain from hate speech and inflammatory rhetoric as the country prepares for the planned December 2026 general elections. Yakani, who heads the Community Empowerment for Progress Organization (CEPO), urged the public and political actors to conduct election-related debates peacefully and responsibly despite differing opinions […] The post Yakani urges South Sudanese to reject hate speech ahead of 2026 elections appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing political instability since its independence in 2011, exacerbated by a civil war that lasted from 2013 to 2018, resulting in significant loss of life and displacement. The Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, signed in September 2018, aimed to establish peace and prepare for elections, but tensions remain high. With the upcoming December 2026 elections, the call for peaceful discourse is crucial to avoid rekindling past hostilities.

articles.title: “Feeling like a superstar today” – Single Dee celebrates second AFRIMMA nomination
articles.summary: Popular South Sudanese musician Deng James, also known as Single Dee, has been nominated for the 2026 African Muzik Magazine Awards (AFRIMMA), earning his second nomination at one of Africa’s leading music award platforms. Single Dee is competing in the Best Male East Africa Artist category alongside Tanzania’s Diamond Platnumz, Kenya’s Bien, Uganda’s Joshua Baraka […] The post “Feeling like a superstar today” – Single Dee celebrates second AFRIMMA nomination appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing conflict since its independence in 2011, with civil war erupting in 2013 and lasting until a peace agreement was signed in 2018. The music industry has become a vital platform for cultural expression and unity amidst the turmoil. Single Dee's nomination at the AFRIMMA highlights the resilience of South Sudanese artists and their ability to gain recognition on an international stage, fostering a sense of national pride and hope for the future.

articles.title: Inside intrigues as Sifuna dropped from Senate whip seat
articles.summary: Chaos, claims of a State House plot and a disputed vote.
articles.context: The recent removal of Edwin Sifuna from the Senate whip position comes amid heightened political tensions following the 2022 general elections, where the opposition coalition, Azimio la Umoja, has struggled to maintain unity against President William Ruto's Kenya Kwanza administration. Allegations of State House interference echo the historical power struggles between the presidency and opposition parties, particularly after the contentious 2018 handshake between President Uhuru Kenyatta and opposition leader Raila Odinga, which reshaped political alliances.

articles.title: Govt delegation leaves for Qatar with Kiir’s condolences over former Emir’s death
articles.summary: A high-level government delegation has travelled to Doha to deliver President Salva Kiir’s message of condolence following the death of Qatar’s former Emir, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani. The delegation, led by Presidential Adviser on National Security Affairs Tut Gatluak Manime and accompanied by Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation Dr. James Pitia […] The post Govt delegation leaves for Qatar with Kiir’s condolences over former Emir’s death appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The death of Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani on September 23, 2023, marks a significant moment for Qatar, a key player in the Horn of Africa's political landscape. South Sudan has historically sought Qatari support for peacebuilding efforts, particularly following the 2018 revitalized peace agreement aimed at ending the civil war. President Salva Kiir's condolences reflect ongoing diplomatic ties and South Sudan's reliance on Qatari mediation in regional conflicts.

articles.title: Build businesses instead of waiting for jobs, youth urged
articles.summary: Young Kenyans urged to embrace entrepreneurship by building small enterprises.
articles.context: In Kenya, youth unemployment has reached alarming levels, with the World Bank reporting a rate of over 20% among young people as of 2022. This economic strain is exacerbated by political instability following the contentious 2022 presidential elections, which have left many disillusioned with traditional job markets. In response, initiatives like the Kenya Youth Employment and Opportunities Project aim to foster entrepreneurship, making this call for youth-led business creation particularly urgent and relevant.

articles.title: Kiir inaugurates JIA Terminal 3, cites ‘positive signal to the world’
articles.summary: President Salva Kiir on Thursday officially inaugurated Terminal 3 at Juba International Airport, marking the completion of a project that took 17 years to build and describing it as a significant step in South Sudan’s efforts to modernise its aviation sector. The new international passenger terminal is equipped with modern facilities, including VIP, business and […] The post Kiir inaugurates JIA Terminal 3, cites ‘positive signal to the world’ appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The inauguration of Terminal 3 at Juba International Airport comes amid ongoing efforts to stabilize South Sudan following years of civil conflict that began in 2013, leading to a peace agreement in 2018. This development is seen as a crucial step for President Salva Kiir's government to attract foreign investment and improve international relations, particularly as the country seeks to recover from economic challenges and enhance its infrastructure.

articles.title: Q&A| MSF: Drone attacks in Sudan’s El Obeid impacting basic services
articles.summary: The medical charity Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF)/Doctors Without Borders has said that the humanitarian situation The post Q&A| MSF: Drone attacks in Sudan’s El Obeid impacting basic services appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: El Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan, has been significantly affected by the ongoing conflict in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. This violence has led to widespread displacement and a collapse of basic services, exacerbating the humanitarian crisis. The recent surge in drone attacks has further destabilized the region, hindering humanitarian efforts by organizations like Médecins Sans Frontières.

articles.title: والي كسلا يلتقي بمفوضي العون الإنساني بالولايات الشرقية
articles.summary: التقى والي كسلا المكلف اللواء ركن (م) الصادق محمد الأزرق على هامش مؤتمر مفوضيات العون الانساني الولايات الشرقية (كسلا- القضارف- البحر الأحمر) المنعقد بولاية كسلا بمفوضي الولايات الشرقية وذلك في إطار تعزيز التنسيق وتبادل الخبرات في القضايا الإنسانية والتنموية. وأعرب مفوض العون الإنساني بولاية البحر الأحمر علي محمد الأمين حسين عن شكره لحكومة ولاية كسلا …
articles.context: تأتي هذه اللقاءات في وقت حساس حيث تواجه ولايات الشرق السوداني، بما في ذلك كسلا والقضارف والبحر الأحمر، تحديات إنسانية متزايدة نتيجة النزاعات المستمرة والأزمات الاقتصادية. منذ اندلاع النزاع في 2021، تفاقمت الأوضاع الإنسانية، مما يستدعي تعزيز التنسيق بين المفوضيات. كما أن حكومة كسلا تسعى لتعزيز التعاون مع المفوضيات الأخرى لتلبية احتياجات السكان المتضررين وتحسين الاستجابة الإنسانية.

articles.title: وزير الموارد البشرية يبحث مع المركز الإفريقي للتدريب والبحث الإداري (كافراد) تعزيز التعاون في الإصلاح الإداري وبناء القدرات
articles.summary: بحث وزير الموارد البشرية والرعاية الاجتماعية الأستاذ معتصم أحمد صالح، مع المدير العام للمركز الإفريقي للتدريب والبحث الإداري للإنماء (كافراد) الدكتور كوفي ديدونيه أسوفي، سبل تعزيز التعاون المشترك في مجالات الإصلاح الإداري وتنمية الموارد البشرية وبناء القدرات والتحول الرقمي. وجاء اللقاء اليوم على هامش أعمال المنتدى الوزاري الإفريقي السابع عشر لتحديث الإدارة العمومية المنعقد بالعاصمة …
articles.context: The meeting between Sudan's Minister of Human Resources, Mutasim Ahmed Saleh, and Dr. Kofi Didoné Asoufi of the African Training and Research Centre in Administration for Development (CAFRAD) comes amid Sudan's ongoing political turmoil following the 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir. The focus on administrative reform and capacity building is crucial as the transitional government seeks to stabilize and modernize governance structures while navigating internal conflicts and international pressures for democratic reforms.

articles.title: Punda wangu: How innovation is bringing Turkana's stolen donkeys home
articles.summary: Hub allows residents to report donkey thefts, disease outbreaks and animal welfare concerns.
articles.context: In recent years, Turkana County has faced escalating conflicts over livestock, particularly donkeys, due to resource scarcity exacerbated by climate change and inter-communal tensions. The 2019 National Drought Management Authority report highlighted a surge in livestock theft, prompting local leaders to seek innovative solutions. The establishment of a reporting hub in 2023 aims to empower communities to combat these issues, fostering collaboration among residents, local authorities, and NGOs to enhance animal welfare and security.

articles.title: مكافحة المخدرات بسنار تضبط مواد مخدرة وتلقي القبض على متهم ينشط في ترويجها
articles.summary: تمكنت شرطة مكافحة المخدرات بولاية سنار اليوم من الإطاحة بمتهم ينشط في ترويج المواد المخدرة بمنطقة (ود النيل) . وقال مدير مكافحة المخدرات بسنار المقدم شرطة قاسم عبد الرحيم في تصريح (لسونا) أن تفاصيل الضبطية تعود إلى عملية رصد ومتابعة ميدانية دقيقة أسفرت عن توقيف المتهم وضبطه وبحوزته (4) أكياس من مخدر (الشاشمندي) إضافة إلى …
articles.context: The recent crackdown on drug trafficking in Sudan, particularly in regions like Senar, comes amid escalating concerns over the rising influence of organized crime and drug cartels in the country. Following the 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir, Sudan has faced instability, which has allowed illicit activities to flourish. The transitional government is under pressure to restore law and order, making drug enforcement a critical focus for maintaining public safety and governance.

articles.title: وزيرة الصناعة والتجارة تلتقي وزير الصناعة والمعادن الليبي على هامش أعمال الجمعية العامة للمنظمة العربية للتنمية الصناعية
articles.summary: التقت الاستاذة محاسن علي يعقوب – وزيرة الصناعة والتجارة، امس بالسيد وزير الصناعة والمعادن الليبي السيد محمد عبد القادر، وذلك في العاصمة الليبية طرابلس بحضور وفدي البلدين، وسفير السودان لدى ليبيا السيد السفير الفريق اول ركن دكتور / إبراهيم محمد أحمد، والسيد السفير / عصام ادريس – نائب رئيس البعثة. وعبرت وزيرة الصناعة والتجارة الاستاذة …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الزيارة في وقت حساس حيث تسعى ليبيا لاستعادة استقرارها الاقتصادي بعد سنوات من النزاع الداخلي، بينما يواجه السودان تحديات سياسية واقتصادية كبيرة بعد الثورة في 2019. التعاون بين البلدين في مجال الصناعة يمكن أن يسهم في تعزيز العلاقات الثنائية، خاصة مع وجود اتفاقيات سابقة مثل اتفاقية التعاون الاقتصادي الموقعة في 2009. كما أن وجود السفير السوداني في ليبيا يعكس أهمية هذه العلاقات في ظل الأزمات الإقليمية.

articles.title: والي كسلا يستقبل وفد الطواف الاداري لجمعية القرآن الكريم
articles.summary: استقبل والي كسلا المكلف اللواء ركن (م) الصادق محمد الازرق وفد الطواف الاداري لجمعية القرآن الكريم الذي يغطي عشر ولايات برئاسة الدكتور علي الزين نائب الامين العام للجمعية، والذي اوضح ان الزيارة الى ولاية كسلا تأتي في اطار جولة الطواف على الولايات بهدف عقد لقاءات ادارية مع منسوبي الجمعية على مستوى الولاية ومحلياتها بالاضافة الى …
articles.context: The visit by the administrative delegation of the Quran Association to Kassala comes at a time of heightened focus on community engagement and religious education in Sudan, particularly following the 2021 coup that disrupted governance. The association's efforts to strengthen ties in various states, including Kassala, reflect ongoing attempts to promote stability and unity among diverse communities amidst the country's political turmoil and economic challenges.

articles.title: علماء روس يطورون تقنية جديدة تمنع انفجار بطاريات الهواتف الذكية
articles.summary: أفاد المكتب الإعلامي لـ”المؤسسة الروسية للعلوم” أن غشاءً جديدًا طوّره علماء من جامعة سان بطرسبورغ، قادر على منع انفجار بطارية الهاتف الذكي في حال ارتفاع درجة حرارتها أو تلفها، وسيمنع هذا الغشاء انبعاث الحرارة والغازات أثناء حدوث ماس كهربائي داخلي، والذي قد يحدث في ظل ظروف تشغيل قاسية للجهاز.وتعدّ بطاريات الليثيوم أيون في الهواتف الذكية …
articles.context: تأتي هذه التقنية الجديدة في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تحديات كبيرة في مجال التكنولوجيا والبنية التحتية، خاصة بعد الصراعات المستمرة منذ عام 2019. تزايد الاعتماد على الهواتف الذكية في ظل الظروف الاقتصادية الصعبة، مما يجعل الابتكارات مثل هذه ذات أهمية خاصة لحماية المستخدمين. كما أن التعاون بين روسيا والسودان في مجالات التكنولوجيا قد يتعزز في ظل الظروف الحالية، مما يفتح آفاقًا جديدة للتعاون الثنائي.

articles.title: Rising dollar worsens cost of living crisis in Juba
articles.summary: As the South Sudanese Pound continues to depreciate against the US dollar, ordinary citizens are The post Rising dollar worsens cost of living crisis in Juba appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The South Sudanese Pound has been in a state of decline since the country's independence in 2011, exacerbated by ongoing conflict and political instability, particularly following the civil war that erupted in 2013. The recent economic crisis has been intensified by the 2020 peace agreement, which, while aimed at unifying warring factions, has failed to stabilize the economy or curb inflation, leading to a severe cost of living crisis for ordinary citizens in Juba.

articles.title: مؤشرات على تنسيق بين الحوثيين وحركة الشباب الصومالية للسيطرة على مضيق باب المندب – في التلغراف
articles.summary: لم يسبق أبداً أن أُغلق المضيقان – هرمز وباب المندب - في نفس الوقت، وقد أظهر الحوثيون قُدرة على إغلاق باب المندب إبان حرب غزة.
articles.context: تعتبر مضيق باب المندب نقطة استراتيجية حيوية تربط البحر الأحمر بخليج عدن، وقد شهدت المنطقة توترات متزايدة بسبب الصراع المستمر في اليمن بين الحوثيين المدعومين من إيران والحكومة اليمنية. في عام 2021، أبرمت حركة الشباب الصومالية اتفاقات مع جماعات مسلحة أخرى لتعزيز نفوذها في المنطقة، مما يثير القلق من إمكانية تنسيقها مع الحوثيين لتهديد الملاحة الدولية في المضيق.

articles.title: UJoSS: Journalists need protection to ensure credible elections
articles.summary: The Union of Journalists of South Sudan (UJOSS) on Wednesday called for stronger protection of The post UJoSS: Journalists need protection to ensure credible elections appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: South Sudan is preparing for its first elections since gaining independence in 2011, amid ongoing political tensions and violence. The UJOSS's call for journalist protection comes in the wake of the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement, which aimed to stabilize the country but has seen limited implementation. With the elections scheduled for 2024, ensuring a safe environment for journalists is crucial for credible reporting and public trust in the electoral process.

articles.title: Shamim Malende Accuses Human Rights Commission Of Failing Ugandans As She Challenges Attorney General’s Defence
articles.summary: Kampala Woman MP Shamim Malende has questioned the effectiveness of the Uganda Human Rights Commission (UHRC), accusing the The post Shamim Malende Accuses Human Rights Commission Of Failing Ugandans As She Challenges Attorney General’s Defence appeared first on The Nile Post .
articles.context: Shamim Malende's criticism of the Uganda Human Rights Commission comes amid heightened scrutiny of human rights abuses in Uganda, particularly following the violent crackdown on opposition protests in 2020. The ongoing political tension between the ruling National Resistance Movement and opposition parties, exemplified by the arrest of prominent figures like Bobi Wine, has intensified calls for accountability and reform within institutions meant to protect citizens' rights.

articles.title: Unity State launches 92-kilometer road project linking Mayom to Warrap
articles.summary: The Governor of Unity State, Joseph Nguen Manytuil, has launched the construction of a 92-kilometer road linking Mayom County to Twic County in neighbouring Warrap State. According to a statement from the Office of the Governor, the road will connect Mankien Payam in Mayom County, Unity State, to Ajakuach Payam in Warrap State. The state […] The post Unity State launches 92-kilometer road project linking Mayom to Warrap appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The road project comes amid ongoing efforts to enhance infrastructure in South Sudan, particularly following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at ending years of civil conflict. Improved connectivity between Unity State and Warrap State is crucial for fostering economic development and stability in a region still recovering from violence and displacement. The initiative also reflects Governor Joseph Nguen Manytuil's commitment to strengthening inter-state relations and promoting trade between the two states.

articles.title: مدير عام الصحة بالقضارف يدشن انطلاقة المسح التغذوي بالولاية
articles.summary: دشن الدكتور أحمد الأمين آدم مدير عام وزارة الصحة بولاية القضارف اليوم انطلاقة مشروع المسح التغذوي بتنفيذ من منظمة صندوق اعانة المرضى بالتعاون مع وزارة الصحة بالولاية ودعم من منظمة اليونسيف والذي يستهدف اربع محليات (باسندا وقلع النحل والرهد والفشقة) وذلك للأطفال دون سن الخامسة والنساء. وأكد الدكتور أحمد الأمين آدم خلال مخاطبته الاحتفال أهمية …
articles.context: تأتي انطلاقة مشروع المسح التغذوي في ولاية القضارف في وقت تعاني فيه السودان من أزمة إنسانية متفاقمة بسبب النزاعات المستمرة، خاصة في مناطق مثل الفشقة. منذ اندلاع النزاع في 2019، تأثرت الخدمات الصحية بشكل كبير، مما جعل التدخلات الإنسانية، مثل هذا المسح، ضرورية لتقييم احتياجات الأطفال والنساء. تدعم منظمة اليونسيف هذا المشروع في إطار جهودها لتحسين الوضع الصحي في البلاد.

articles.title: توجيهات بالالتزام بإنفاذ مشروعات التنمية بالخرطوم وفقا للجدولة الزمنية المحددة
articles.summary: وجه المدير التنفيذي لمحلية الخرطوم عبد المنعم البشير باستكمال جميع مشروعات التنمية وفقا للجدولة الزمنية المحددة حسب التعاقدات المبرمة مع المستثمرين والمشغلين للمشروعات المختلفة بالمحلية. وشدد البشير خلال تفقده مشروعات الحدائق العامة اليوم برفقة إدارتي الاستثمار والشئون المالية بوضع الترتيبات اللازمة لإنفاذ الشروط المحاسبية مع المستثمرين المتباطئين في إنفاذ المشروعات وفقا للسقف الزمني المحدد لهم …
articles.context: تأتي توجيهات المدير التنفيذي لمحلية الخرطوم في سياق جهود الحكومة المحلية لتعزيز التنمية الاقتصادية في ظل الأزمات المستمرة التي تواجه السودان، بما في ذلك النزاع المسلح في مناطق مثل دارفور. منذ اتفاق جوبا للسلام في أكتوبر 2020، تسعى الحكومة إلى تنفيذ مشروعات تنموية لتحسين الظروف المعيشية، لكن التباطؤ في التنفيذ من قبل بعض المستثمرين يهدد هذه الجهود.

articles.title: السودان يدعو مجلس الأمن لتسريع إجراءات الجنائية الدولية بشأن جرائم دارفور ويحذر من الإفلات من العقاب
